purpose: Per-task outcome record for the TASK-HMIG-010 feature-level LangGraph validation run. Populated by the operator after each task completes (the runner does not yet auto-emit this — see TASK-FIX-CANARY-PARSER for the analogous gap on the canary side).

run_attempts.notes: Aborted at Wave 1 / TASK-FIX-IA03 / Turn 1 with LangGraphHarnessError (auth). Root cause: feature subcommand doesn't thread --model. See incident I-001 (F9) in feature-run-incidents.md and TASK-FIX-LGFM.

run_attempts.halted_by: stop_on_failure=True after Wave 1 task 1 errored on turn 2
run_attempts.notes: Post-LGFM run. Surfaced F10 (main Player still gets model=None — sibling-of-F9 in AgentInvoker._invoke_task_work_implement, line 5730) and F11 (DeepAgents conversation_history offload to read-only /conversation_history/ host root, sibling-of-NOVMODE). Both filed as TASK-FIX-LGFM2 and TASK-FIX-CHO01. Coach successfully ran for ~1.5min on synthetic report (with model='openai:qwen36-workhorse' — proving Coach side of model threading is correct), but main Player path consumed 2 turns failing identically. See I-002 and I-003 in incidents.

task_outcomes.notes: Failed at Player turn 1 with LangGraphHarnessError(model=None, auth). Not a substrate-quality finding — this is a CLI-plumbing gap (F9 / TASK-FIX-LGFM). After LGFM lands, re-run with --fresh.

Post-LGFM run. Main Player still fails with model=None (F10, AgentInvoker._invoke_task_work_implement missing model= threading). Synthetic report fired; test-orchestrator specialist DID get model='openai:qwen36-workhorse' (proving the Coach + specialist paths got LGFM correctly), but immediately hit F11 (DeepAgents conversation_history offload fails to read-only host root /conversation_history/, summarization can't trim, prompt grows to 569k tokens, llama-swap returns 400 exceed_context_size). Two new blockers filed: TASK-FIX-LGFM2 (F10) and TASK-FIX-CHO01 (F11).

task_outcome_schema.first_pass_success: true|false (bool, required) — true iff coach_decision == 'approve' AND turns_used == 1
task_outcome_schema.resume_used: true|false (bool, required) — true iff a --resume invocation was needed
task_outcome_schema.non_recoverable: true|false (bool, required) — true iff failure required code edits to the harness itself per AC-006 definition
task_outcome_schema.notes: free-form string, optional — failure analysis, resume rationale, divergence-from-canary commentary
